threats.title: Session hijacking or credential stuffing
threats.description: An attacker rides a stolen session cookie or replays leaked credentials against the login. Weak session handling or a missing rate limit makes it cheap.

threats.title: Cross-site scripting injects script into a page
threats.description: Untrusted input reaches the DOM or an HTML response without contextual encoding, so the attacker runs script in the victim's session.

threats.title: Security-relevant actions are not attributable
threats.description: Auth events and state changes are unlogged or the log is forgeable (CRLF injection), so an actor can deny what they did.

threats.title: Sensitive data leaks via responses or errors
threats.description: Verbose errors, debug endpoints, or secrets in client-side storage expose data the user should not see.

threats.title: Resource exhaustion from unbounded input
threats.description: A pathological regular expression or an unbounded request body ties up a worker (ReDoS, decompression), degrading availability.

threats.title: Broken access control lets a user act out of scope
threats.description: A route or object is reachable without the authorization check that should gate it, so a user reaches another tenant's data or an admin action.
